entries.label: Basic Result
entries.note: Result has two variants: Ok(value) and Err(error)

entries.label: Match on Result
entries.note: Exhaustive handling of both success and failure

entries.label: unwrap and expect
entries.code:
let val = divide(10.0, 2.0).unwrap();       // panics on Err
let val = divide(10.0, 2.0).expect("oops"); // panics with message
entries.note: Quick and dirty; panics on error. Use in tests or when you are certain

entries.label: unwrap_or variants
entries.note: Provide a fallback value instead of panicking

entries.label: is_ok / is_err
entries.note: Check variant without consuming the Result

entries.label: Propagate errors
entries.note: ? returns early with Err; unwraps Ok. Function must return Result

entries.label: ? with Option
entries.note: ? works with Option too; returns None early

entries.label: ? in main
entries.note: main can return Result for using ? at the top level

entries.label: Chaining with ?
entries.note: Chain multiple fallible operations cleanly

entries.label: Basic Option
entries.note: Option represents a value that may or may not exist

entries.label: Pattern matching
entries.note: Handle both Some and None variants

entries.label: if let
entries.note: Shorthand when you only care about the Some case

entries.label: unwrap_or variants
entries.note: Provide defaults for None

entries.label: Convert Option to Result
entries.note: ok_or converts None to Err; useful with ? operator

entries.label: map
entries.note: Transform the success/some value; error/none passes through

entries.label: and_then (flatmap)
entries.note: Chain operations that themselves return Result/Option

entries.label: map_err
entries.code:
let result: Result<i32, String> = Err("oops".into());
let mapped = result.map_err(|e| format!("Error: {e}"));
entries.note: Transform the error value; Ok passes through

entries.label: or_else
entries.code:
let result: Result<i32, String> = Err("fail".into());
let fallback = result.or_else(|_| Ok(0));
entries.note: Provide an alternative Result/Option on failure

entries.label: filter (Option)
entries.code:
let even = Some(4).filter(|x| x % 2 == 0); // Some(4)
let odd = Some(3).filter(|x| x % 2 == 0);  // None
entries.note: Keep Some only if predicate is true

entries.label: flatten
entries.note: Remove one level of nesting

entries.label: zip (Option)
entries.note: Combine two Options into a tuple; None if either is None

entries.label: Simple custom error
entries.note: Implement Display and Error trait for custom errors

entries.label: From for error conversion
entries.note: Implement From to enable automatic conversion with ?

entries.label: Box<dyn Error>
entries.note: Easiest approach; accepts any error type. Less type information.

entries.label: Define errors
entries.note: Derive macro generates Display and Error impls

entries.label: Source chain
entries.note: #[source] sets the error source for chaining

entries.label: #[from] for auto-conversion
entries.note: #[from] generates From impl for automatic conversion

entries.label: Basic usage
entries.note: anyhow::Result<T> = Result<T, anyhow::Error>; accepts any error

entries.label: Add context
entries.note: context/with_context add human-readable messages to error chain

entries.label: anyhow! and bail!
entries.note: anyhow! creates error; bail! returns early; ensure! is like assert

entries.label: Downcast
entries.note: Recover the original error type when needed

entries.label: panic!
entries.code: panic!("something went terribly wrong");
entries.note: Unrecoverable error; unwinds the stack and terminates

entries.label: catch_unwind
entries.note: Catches panics; returns Result. Not for normal error handling

entries.label: When to panic
entries.note: Rule of thumb: panic for bugs, Result for expected failures
